hypothesis: If the β parameter in DPO controls the degree of distributional shift from the SFT reference policy, then dynamically scheduling β as a function of per-token uncertainty on domain-critical chemistry tokens (element symbols and stoichiometric numerals) during DPO training—increasing β when gradient updates target high-KL chemistry tokens and decreasing it otherwise—will prevent reward-model overoptimization in architecturally sensitive models like OLMo while preserving or improving reward gains in more robust models like LLaMA and Gemma, thereby enabling consistent multi-objective alignment across heterogeneous model architectures without requiring architecture-specific hyperparameter searches.
